medications.indication_rationale: Anti-inflammatory therapy for an RA flare, taken with food, provided no contraindication (renal/GI/cardiac).
medications.dose_route_frequency: e.g. ibuprofen 400 mg PO with food up to TDS as needed (adult, if no contraindication)

medications.indication_rationale: Additional simple analgesia, especially if NSAIDs are contraindicated.
medications.dose_route_frequency: paracetamol 1 g PO up to QID as needed (adult)

medications.indication_rationale: No evidence of joint infection; antibiotics are not indicated.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Rest the hands tomorrow, use warm-water soaks with gentle fist opening/closing, and take the NSAID with food.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reduces stiffness and inflammation and speeds recovery.

patient_education_points.point: This is a flare of your mild inflammatory arthritis brought on by overusing your hands — the joint lining is temporarily inflamed, not the bones crumbling. Mild seronegative RA rarely causes rapid, permanent deformity.
Use an anti-inflammatory with food, warm-water soaks, and rest your hands tomorrow.
Go to ED if ONE joint suddenly becomes bright red, hot, and agony to move even slightly, or you develop a high fever with chills.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Flare resolution, long-term NSAID/DMARD plan, rheumatology referral.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Single hot red joint, fever, or spread to large joints with persistent swelling.

must_include_items: Screen for a single hot red joint/fever (septic arthritis) before reassuring
Offer NSAIDs (with food, checking contraindications) + warmth + rest
Reassure that mild seronegative RA rarely causes rapid deformity
errors_of_commission: ED escalation for a symmetric afebrile flare
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: T2 case: passing requires a septic-arthritis screen + symptomatic management + safety-net. ED over-triage of a symmetric afebrile flare is a failure.
